title: Heli the Rescue Helicopter and the Mountain Picnic
description: Heli carries a picnic basket over a blocked mountain trail and finds a calm path through the mist.

Heli.role: Main character; a careful, kind mountain rescue helicopter
Heli.color: Sunshine-yellow rounded body with a white nose, sky-blue lower stripe, and coral-red tail tip
Heli.bodyShape: Compact friendly helicopter with a bulbous cabin, short tail boom, one large four-blade charcoal rotor, and no arms or hands
Heli.face: Two large warm-brown windshield eyes with soft brows and a small friendly smile below the glass
Heli.landingGear: Two short silver skids with dark charcoal foot pads
Heli.accessories: Small blue rescue beacon, silver side mirrors, round sky-blue heart emblem, and a blue cargo sling carried only on delivery pages

Piper.role: Supporting character; a practical little park ranger pickup
Piper.appearance: Forest-green compact pickup with cream roof, amber windshield eyes, tan bumpers, four black tires, and a small pine-tree emblem with no readable text

Mountain friends.appearance: One small white mountain goat with gray horns and one cinnamon-brown rabbit with long upright ears

settingBible.Sunrise Valley Rescue Pad: A round pale-stone helipad beside a tiny red-roofed rescue shed, wildflowers, pine trees, and distant blue mountains
settingBible.Lookout Meadow: A high green meadow with purple lupines, smooth gray rocks, a little waterfall, and a wide view across layered mountains
settingBible.visualStyle: Warm polished 3D children's illustration with rounded forms, soft cinematic light, clean single-scene composition, and no readable text

continuityRules: Heli keeps the same yellow, white, sky-blue, and coral-red color blocking on every page.
Heli always has warm-brown windshield eyes, four charcoal rotor blades, silver skids, blue beacon, side mirrors, and one sky-blue heart emblem.
Heli and every vehicle have no human-like arms or hands.
Piper remains forest green with a cream roof, tan bumpers, amber windshield eyes, four matching tires, and a pine-tree emblem.
The picnic basket is woven honey-brown with a coral cloth tucked beneath its lid and hangs from the same blue cargo sling on Pages 5-11.
The mountain goat remains small and white with gray horns; the rabbit remains cinnamon brown with upright ears.
The day progresses from clear sunrise to bright morning, pearly mist, warm afternoon, sunset, and calm moonlit night.
Every illustration is a single full-bleed 4:3 scene with the bottom 25-30 percent reserved for low-detail scenery and no text, badge, panel, logo, watermark, collage, or grid.

pages.text: Early one sunny morning, Heli the Rescue Helicopter woke at the Sunrise Valley rescue pad. His yellow body gleamed.
pages.illustrationScene: Heli rests on the pale-stone rescue pad at sunrise, with the red-roofed shed, flowers, pines, and distant mountains behind him.
pages.continuityNotes: Establish Heli's exact yellow body, white nose, blue lower stripe, coral tail tip, brown windshield eyes, charcoal rotor, silver skids, beacon, mirrors, and heart emblem.

pages.text: Piper the Park Ranger Pickup rolled in with a picnic basket. “The meadow trail is blocked, and our mountain friends need help,” she said.
pages.illustrationScene: Piper arrives beside Heli carrying the woven picnic basket in her open truck bed while a small safe rockfall is visible far up the empty trail.
pages.continuityNotes: Introduce Piper's green body, cream roof, amber eyes, tan bumpers, black tires, and pine emblem. Show the honey-brown basket with coral cloth.

pages.text: Heli looked toward the high green meadow. “I can carry the picnic safely over the rocks,” he promised.
pages.illustrationScene: Heli and Piper look toward Lookout Meadow high above the blocked trail, with Heli smiling confidently and the basket between them.
pages.continuityNotes: Keep both vehicles at established scale; Heli remains grounded and the basket is not attached yet.

pages.text: First, Heli checked the breeze, his rotor, and the clear blue sky. Everything was ready for a gentle flight.
pages.illustrationScene: Heli performs a calm preflight check on the rescue pad as a windsock moves lightly and Piper waits beside the basket.
pages.continuityNotes: Rotor is still or turning very slowly; show no hands, tools, readable signs, or extra rescue gear.

pages.text: Piper fastened the basket into Heli’s blue cargo sling. Heli lifted it smoothly above the soft grass.
pages.illustrationScene: Heli hovers low above the pad with the picnic basket secured in a blue sling below his cabin while Piper watches proudly from a safe distance.
pages.continuityNotes: Introduce the blue sling and keep the basket closed, level, and clearly below Heli; place all subjects well above the bottom safe zone.

pages.text: Up Heli flew above the pine forest. The little basket swayed as softly as a sleepy swing.
pages.illustrationScene: Heli flies over rounded pine treetops with the secured basket hanging steadily beneath him and the valley rescue pad shrinking behind.
pages.continuityNotes: Keep Heli and the full sling visible against open sky; basket remains closed with coral cloth tucked inside.

pages.text: Near the mountain, a ribbon of pearly mist drifted across Heli’s usual route. Heli slowed and hovered.
pages.illustrationScene: Heli pauses before a gentle band of pearly mist between two pine-covered slopes, with the basket steady beneath him.
pages.continuityNotes: Mist is calm and nonthreatening; Heli stays outside it with alert brown eyes and unchanged colors.

pages.text: Heli listened for the little waterfall beside Lookout Meadow. Splash and sparkle showed him a clearer way.
pages.illustrationScene: Heli turns toward a visible silver waterfall beyond the mist, following a sunlit gap while the blue sling remains vertical.
pages.continuityNotes: Show the waterfall as the new landmark and maintain the same basket, sling, rotor, skids, and heart emblem.

pages.text: Heli followed the waterfall around the misty ridge. Beyond the last pine tree, warm sunlight opened ahead.
pages.illustrationScene: Heli rounds the final pine-covered ridge beside the waterfall, leaving mist behind as bright meadow light appears beyond the frame direction of travel.
pages.continuityNotes: Bridge naturally into Page 10: waterfall and final pine mark the ridge, with open sunlit meadow immediately ahead.

pages.text: There was Lookout Meadow, bright with purple flowers. The little goat and rabbit waved from a clear landing spot.
pages.illustrationScene: Heli emerges from beside the same waterfall into Lookout Meadow where the white goat and cinnamon rabbit wait near a clear grassy landing area.
pages.continuityNotes: Continue Page 9 geography with waterfall behind Heli; introduce the goat and rabbit at consistent small scale above the safe zone.

pages.text: Heli lowered the basket onto the meadow together with one careful hover. The picnic arrived safe and snug.
pages.illustrationScene: Heli hovers over the clear meadow as the basket gently touches the grass, with the goat and rabbit watching from a safe distance.
pages.continuityNotes: Basket is still in the blue sling at touchdown; keep rotor, skids, faces, friends, and basket above the text-safe boundary.

pages.text: The goat opened the basket while the rabbit spread the coral picnic cloth. Sweet berries and tiny sandwiches were ready to share.
pages.illustrationScene: The goat and rabbit arrange berries and small sandwiches on the coral cloth in the meadow while Heli rests nearby and the empty blue sling is neatly reeled up.
pages.continuityNotes: Basket is open, coral cloth is spread, and Heli's sling is no longer hanging; keep food simple and all key objects above the safe zone.

pages.text: Piper reached the meadow by the newly cleared trail. Everyone cheered for Heli’s calm and clever flight.
pages.illustrationScene: Piper rolls into Lookout Meadow on the cleared trail as Heli, the goat, and the rabbit greet her beside the picnic.
pages.continuityNotes: Reintroduce Piper unchanged; show the cleared trail without workers or machinery, and keep the picnic arrangement consistent.

pages.text: As the sun turned peach and gold, Heli flew home above the quiet pines. Lookout Meadow glowed behind him.
pages.illustrationScene: Heli flies alone toward Sunrise Valley at sunset, with peach clouds, darkening pines below, and the glowing meadow far behind.
pages.continuityNotes: No basket or sling hangs beneath Heli; preserve every locked feature in warm sunset light.

pages.text: That night, Heli rested beneath a silver moon. “Goodnight, mountain friends,” he whispered, and closed his sleepy eyes.
pages.illustrationScene: Heli rests on the Sunrise Valley rescue pad beneath a silver crescent moon, eyes closed, with the little shed warmly lit and mountains sleeping beyond.
pages.continuityNotes: Return to the Page 1 rescue pad; Heli has no sling, keeps all locked features, and ends in a calm bedtime pose.
